name: Charis Transportation
recent_news: NEMT van provider in Vallejo, CA; NPI #1598047144 registered as Non-emergency Medical Transport (VAN). No school-district contracts identified.
fit_notes: Not a school-bus operator. Pure NEMT/wheelchair-van micro-business (~6 employees, ~$400K revenue). Out of scope for Summit tuck-in; no SPED route exposure indicated.

name: Active Transportation
districts_or_customers: Northeast Ohio NEMT clients; no school district contracts identified
recent_news: State-regulated NEMT/ambulette company operating since 2010 throughout NE Ohio; serves elderly and disabled, not students. BBB-listed in Fairlawn, OH.
fit_notes: Not a school-bus contractor. NEMT/ambulette only; no SPED or yellow-bus exposure. Out of scope for Summit.

name: Direct Point Logistics Inc
recent_news: FMCSA SAFER shows USDOT 2384017 / MC-817054 as an active BROKER entity with 0 power units; address tied to Brentwood, TN registration despite IL listing. No passenger or school operations.
fit_notes: Not a school-bus operator at all. Non-asset freight broker per FMCSA (0 trucks). Completely out of scope for Summit.

name: Siebert Mobility Of Iowa LLC
owner_or_parent: independent (affiliated with Siebert Mobility Inc., founded 1978 by Bill Siebert)
principals: Bill Siebert (founder of parent Siebert Mobility Inc., 1978)
districts_or_customers: individual mobility consumers; not school districts
recent_news: Iowa City retail dealership for wheelchair-accessible vans (Braun Entervan, BraunAbility, VMI), lifts, hand controls and stairlifts. Pure mobility-equipment dealer, not a transportation operator.
fit_notes: Not an operator. Retail mobility-van dealer/upfitter. Zero relevance to Summit tuck-in strategy.

name: Windy City Limo-Bus Worldwide
districts_or_customers: Corporate, hospitality and event clients in Chicagoland; no K-12 school district route contracts identified
recent_news: Founded 2006 by George Jacobs (former owner of American Limousine). Large Chicagoland chauffeured/luxury motorcoach operator with sedans, SUVs, vans, minibuses and 40-56 pax coaches. Listed employee count of 5 at the Broadview address appears to drastically understate true scale (DB likely captures a sub-entity).
fit_notes: Not a school-bus contractor. Luxury/charter operator; 'student' product is after-event chauffeured ride-home service, not K-12 home-to-school routes. Out of scope for Summit tuck-in.
